title: Supreme Court Keeps Abortion Pill Available by Mail
subtitle: A recent court decision ensures that mifepristone remains accessible to patients nationwide for now.

summary.en: The US Supreme Court has allowed the continued use of the abortion pill mifepristone by mail. This decision stops a lower court order that would have banned the drug's delivery. The case began when the state of Louisiana sued the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to change how the drug is prescribed. Currently, medication accounts for nearly two-thirds of all abortions in the United States. The legal battle is expected to continue in the coming months.
summary.zh: 美國最高法院已允許繼續透過郵寄方式使用墮胎藥美服培酮。這項裁決擋下了下級法院原先可能禁止藥物配送的命令。此案源於路易斯安那州對食品藥物管理局（FDA）提起的訴訟，旨在改變該藥物的處方規定。目前，藥物墮胎占美國所有墮胎案例的近三分之二。預計未來幾個月內，這場法律戰仍將持續進行。

paragraphs.en: The United States Supreme Court recently made a major decision regarding access to mifepristone, a common medication used for abortion. In a 7-2 ruling, the court decided that the pill can continue to be sent through the mail to patients. This decision provides a temporary victory for those who support access to the drug, as it blocks a lower court’s order that would have stopped this practice nationwide.
paragraphs.zh: 美國最高法院近期針對墮胎常用藥物「美服培酮」（mifepristone）的取得管道做出重大裁決。法院以七比二的票數決定，該藥物可繼續透過郵寄方式送達患者手中。這項裁決對支持藥物墮胎權的人士而言是一次暫時性的勝利，因為它擋下了下級法院原先可能在全國範圍內禁止此類做法的命令。

paragraphs.en: The case started when the state of Louisiana sued the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA). The FDA is the government agency responsible for ensuring that food and medicine are safe. Louisiana argued that the FDA’s rules for prescribing mifepristone remotely were problematic. Specifically, the state claimed that allowing the drug to be mailed to people in Louisiana interfered with the state’s own local ban on abortion. A lower court, the Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals, had previously agreed with Louisiana, which would have effectively ended mail-order access to the drug across the entire country.
paragraphs.zh: 此案源於路易斯安那州對美國食品藥物管理局（FDA）提起的訴訟。FDA 是負責確保食品與藥物安全的政府機構。路易斯安那州主張，FDA 關於遠距開立美服培酮處方的規定存在問題。具體而言，該州聲稱允許將藥物郵寄給路易斯安那州的民眾，干擾了該州自行實施的墮胎禁令。下級法院「第五巡迴上訴法院」先前曾同意路易斯安那州的觀點，這項判決若執行，將形同終止全國範圍內的藥物郵寄服務。

paragraphs.en: However, the Supreme Court found that Louisiana did not have the legal standing to challenge the FDA’s rules in this way. In legal terms, having "standing" means a person or group has a valid, direct reason to bring a lawsuit to court. Because the court decided the state lacked this standing, the case was sent back to the lower court. This means the current rules will stay in place while the legal process continues. Experts expect the case to return to the Supreme Court for an official appeal in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，最高法院認為路易斯安那州在此案中並不具備挑戰 FDA 規定的法律適格性。在法律術語中，「適格性」意指個人或團體必須具備正當且直接的理由，才能向法院提起訴訟。由於法院裁定該州缺乏適格性，案件被發回下級法院審理。這意味著在法律程序持續進行的同時，現行規定將維持不變。專家預計，此案未來將會再次上訴至最高法院。

paragraphs.en: This is not the first time the Supreme Court has dealt with this issue. In 2022, the court overturned Roe v. Wade, a landmark decision that had protected the right to abortion for decades. Since that time, many states have banned abortion entirely. Because medication abortions now account for nearly two-thirds of all abortions in the U.S., the availability of these pills is a central part of the national debate. According to The Guardian, abortion rates have remained steady in recent years, largely because patients can still receive these medications by mail.
paragraphs.zh: 這並非最高法院首次處理此類議題。二〇二二年，法院推翻了「羅訴韋德案」（Roe v. Wade），這是一項曾保障墮胎權數十年的指標性判決。自那時起，許多州已全面禁止墮胎。由於藥物墮胎目前占美國所有墮胎案例的近三分之二，這些藥物的取得管道已成為全國辯論的核心。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，近年來墮胎率保持穩定，主要原因在於患者仍能透過郵寄方式取得這些藥物。

paragraphs.en: There are strong disagreements regarding this topic. In their dissenting opinions—the written arguments of judges who disagree with the majority—Justices Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito argued against the ruling. Justice Thomas suggested that mailing the pills could be considered a "criminal enterprise." He also pointed to the Comstock Act, an old law from 1873 that prohibited the mailing of materials related to abortion. Justice Alito expressed concern that the current rules are part of a plan to weaken the court’s 2022 decision that ended the federal right to abortion.
paragraphs.zh: 針對此議題，各界存在強烈分歧。在不同意見書中——即持反對意見的法官所撰寫的論點——大法官湯瑪斯（Clarence Thomas）與阿利托（Samuel Alito）對此裁決表示反對。湯瑪斯大法官暗示，郵寄這些藥物可能被視為一種「犯罪事業」。他也提到了《康斯托克法》（Comstock Act），這是一項一八七三年的舊法，禁止郵寄與墮胎相關的物品。阿利托大法官則表達擔憂，認為現行規定是削弱法院二〇二二年終止聯邦墮胎權判決之計畫的一部分。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, the FDA maintains that mifepristone is safe and effective. The agency first approved the drug in 2000 and later removed the requirement for patients to visit a doctor in person to receive it. This change allowed for "telehealth" appointments, where a patient speaks with a doctor over a video call instead of visiting an office. According to STAT News, the FDA has repeatedly reviewed the safety of the medication and found it to be reliable.
paragraphs.zh: 與此同時，FDA 堅稱美服培酮是安全且有效的。該機構於二〇〇〇年首次批准該藥物，隨後取消了患者必須親自就醫才能領藥的規定。這項變革促成了「遠距醫療」門診的出現，患者可以透過視訊通話與醫師諮詢，而無需親自前往診所。根據《STAT 新聞》（STAT News）報導，FDA 已多次審查該藥物的安全性，並認定其可靠。

paragraphs.en: The political environment surrounding this issue is also very tense. STAT News reports that anti-abortion groups have been putting pressure on the government to speed up a new safety review of the drug. These groups hope that a new review will lead to stricter rules, such as stopping the use of telehealth platforms for prescribing the pill. This pressure recently led to the resignation of the FDA Commissioner, Marty Makary, after months of criticism from political leaders.
paragraphs.zh: 圍繞此議題的政治環境也相當緊張。《STAT 新聞》報導指出，反墮胎團體一直向政府施壓，要求加速對該藥物進行新的安全性審查。這些團體希望新的審查能促成更嚴格的規定，例如禁止使用遠距醫療平台來開立該藥物。在經歷數月來自政治領袖的批評後，這股壓力近期導致了 FDA 局長馬卡里（Marty Makary）的辭職。

paragraphs.en: For now, women across the country can still obtain mifepristone through pharmacies or by mail without needing an in-person visit. While the Supreme Court has preserved this access for the time being, the legal battle is far from over. As the case moves through the court system again, the future of medication abortion remains a major point of conflict in American law and politics.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，全國各地的女性仍可透過藥局或郵寄方式取得美服培酮，無需親自就醫。雖然最高法院暫時保留了此項取得管道，但法律戰遠未結束。隨著案件再次進入法院體系，藥物墮胎的未來仍是美國法律與政治中主要的衝突點。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop or prevent something from happening or moving forward.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（街區、積木），這裡作動詞用。文中：it blocks a lower court’s order that would have stopped this practice nationwide.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The legal right to bring a case to a court of law.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（站立），這裡指法律上的資格。文中：However, the Supreme Court found that Louisiana did not have the legal standing to challenge the FDA’s rules in this way.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make up a particular amount or proportion of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 容易誤解為「解釋」，這裡指數量上的佔比。文中：Because medication abortions now account for nearly two-thirds of all abortions in the U.S., the availability of these pills is a central part of the national debate.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Not changing or fluctuating; remaining at the same level.
keyTerms.noteZh: 容易誤解為「平穩的（動作）」，這裡指數據沒有波動。文中：According to The Guardian, abortion rates have remained steady in recent years, largely because patients can still receive these medications by mail.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Supreme court allows abortion pill mifepristone to continue to be available by mail

sources.name: NPR Health
sources.title: Eyes are on the Supreme Court for another abortion pill ruling

sources.name: STAT News
sources.title: Supreme Court preserves access to widely used abortion pill, while lawsuit plays out

quiz.question: According to the article, what was the specific reason the Supreme Court did not uphold the lower court's order to stop the mailing of mifepristone?

options.A: The court decided that the FDA's safety reviews were entirely flawless.
options.B: The court determined that the state of Louisiana lacked the legal standing to bring the lawsuit.
options.C: The court ruled that the Comstock Act of 1873 was no longer a valid law.
options.D: The court concluded that mail-order abortion pills do not violate state bans.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，最高法院認為路易斯安那州缺乏法律上的「standing」（訴訟資格），意即該州沒有正當的法律理由來挑戰 FDA 的規定。選項 A 文中未提及 FDA 審查完美無缺；選項 C 文中僅提到法官引用該法，並未判定其無效；選項 D 則是該州的主張，而非法院的判決理由。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what can be inferred about the future of medication abortion access in the United States?

options.A: The Supreme Court has permanently settled the debate over the safety of the drug.
options.B: Anti-abortion groups have successfully stopped all telehealth prescriptions for the pill.
options.C: The legal conflict is likely to continue because the case will probably return to the Supreme Court.
options.D: The resignation of the FDA Commissioner will immediately result in a ban on mail-order pills.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第三段與最後一段，法院僅是將案件發回下級法院審理，專家預測該案未來會再次上訴至最高法院，且文中強調法律鬥爭遠未結束。選項 A 與事實不符，爭議仍在；選項 B 錯誤，目前郵寄與遠距醫療仍可使用；選項 D 誇大了人事異動對法律程序的直接影響。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'enterprise' mean in the phrase 'criminal enterprise'?

options.A: A bold or difficult project or undertaking.
options.B: An organized system of illegal activities.
options.C: A business organization that provides employment.
options.D: A creative attempt to solve a legal problem.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在文中，大法官 Clarence Thomas 使用「criminal enterprise」來形容郵寄墮胎藥的行為，意指這是一種組織性的犯罪活動。選項 A 是該詞的一般定義，但在本句中帶有強烈的負面法律指控意味；選項 C 與 D 忽略了「criminal」一詞帶來的非法性質，與法官反對的立場不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: The FDA has officially declared that mifepristone is the safest abortion method available.
options.B: The Supreme Court has permanently protected the right to abortion across all 50 states.
options.C: The legal and political battle over the distribution of abortion medication remains ongoing and unresolved.
options.D: Louisiana has successfully convinced the Supreme Court to re-examine the safety of abortion pills.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文的核心在於描述最高法院近期針對郵寄墮胎藥的暫時性裁決，以及圍繞此藥物的法律、政治與社會爭議。文章最後一段明確指出法律戰尚未結束，這正是文章的主旨。選項 A 僅是 FDA 的立場；選項 B 錯誤，法院僅是處理郵寄問題，而非全面保護墮胎權；選項 D 錯誤，路易斯安那州並未成功，法院因資格問題駁回了其主張。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
